steps.title: Find the contrast the sentence sets up
steps.content: The passage states that in 1980 none of the countries exceeded 2 women, then uses “but ______” to signal what changed by 2010. The blank must show how the countries exceeded that 1980 baseline, demonstrating the upward trend mentioned in the opening claim.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Reading 2010 values from the graph: Peru = 3, Philippines = 3, Mexico = 3. Choice A states exactly this, showing all three countries went from ≤2 in 1980 to 3 in 2010—a clear, parallel illustration of the upward trend. B compares which country increased most, but that doesn't respond to the contrast between 1980 and 2010; C incorrectly claims Philippines never dipped (the graph shows it fell from 3 to 2 between 1990 and 2000); D mentions only Mexico, leaving most of the data unused. A completes the example most effectively by using all available data to show the shift from the 1980 baseline.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
